body.title: Expose Scraps embedding_engine as rack MCP tool
body.result: embed_text() on ScrapsDevice + scraps_embed_text MCP tool in librarian registry; hash fallback works without OPENAI_API_KEY; 5 tests
body.intention: I intend that any rack device can request text embeddings by calling scraps_embed_text via MCP without knowing the provider.
body.description:
The embedding engine in devices/scraps/embedding_engine.py computes text→vector (OpenAI text-embedding-3-small primary, hash-sha256 fallback) but is not callable as a rack MCP tool. ScrapsDevice is currently only a ticket gatekeeper. Add embed_text(text, model='auto') → {vector, model, dimension} as a ScrapsDevice method exposed via the rack MCP server.

**Affected files:** devices/scraps/scraps_device.py (add embed_text method), devices/scraps/shim.py (expose via MCP handler if needed), devices/scraps/embedding_engine.py (already implements logic — wiring only)
**Design rules:** OOP-first — add as ScrapsDevice method; no-sqlite; interface logging at every MCP crossing per AR-009
**Scope boundary:** IN: expose embed_text via rack MCP; fallback works when OPENAI_API_KEY unset. OUT: changing embedding models, batch endpoint, any DB writes (caller owns writes per embedding_engine.py design rule), word graph migration (decoupled)
**Completion criteria:** mcp__scraps__embed_text(text='hello world') returns dict with vector + model + dimension; test verifies hash-sha256 fallback fires when OPENAI_API_KEY unset; existing scraps_validate_ticket tests still pass.
